title: The AI jobs that didn't exist 2 years ago (and pay $200K+)
post:
Two years ago, these jobs didn't exist. Today, they're paying $200K+.

1. Prompt Engineer: Crafting AI instructions for specific domains
2. AI Product Manager: Bridging technical AI capabilities with business needs
3. ML Reliability Engineer: Ensuring AI systems stay accurate in production
4. AI Auditor: Evaluating AI systems for bias and safety
5. Agentic Workflow Designer: Building automation pipelines with AI agents
6. Synthetic Data Specialist: Creating training data for AI models

The talent gap is real. Companies are paying premiums for people who understand both the technology and the application.

I'm seeing bootcamp grads with 6 months of AI experience land roles that previously required 10 years.

The window is open. But it won't stay open forever.

Which of these roles interests you most?
